basic_details.job_title: ICAR IARI Part-time Medical Officer Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online
basic_details.short_information: ICAR Indian Agricultural Research Institute (ICAR IARI) Part time Medical Officer Recruitment 2026 Notification has been released on the official website. A total of 1 vacancy has been announced through the ICAR IARI Part time Medical Officer Recruitment 2026. Candidates aged between 18 to 65 years who have MBBS or equivalent degree are eligible to apply for this Part time Medical Officer vacancy. The email application process for ICAR IARI Part time Medical Officer Recruitment 2026 has started and will remain open until 16-06-2026. Candidates can download the official notification PDF and app

age_limit.details: Maximum Age Limit: 65 years (as on 01 June 2026) Age relaxation is relaxable in exceptional cases on a case-to-case basis. However, no engagement shall be permissible beyond 70 years under any circumstances.

qualification.details: MBBS or equivalent degree from a recognized university/institution and should be registered with the National Medical Commission (NMC)/State Medical Council/other statutory body as per legal requirement for practicing medical treatment. Preference may be given to candidates with a minimum of 05 years of relevant experience in a Government/Autonomous/Private hospital setup.

selection_process: Application Screening Walk-in Interview Document Verification
how_to_apply: Candidates who wish to apply for ICAR IARI Part time Medical Officer Recruitment 2026 via email should follow the steps below.
Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Prepare your application with complete bio-data including personal details, educational qualifications, and experience.
Attach self-attested copies of all required documents such as educational certificates, experience certificates, ID proof, and recent passport-size photographs.
Clearly mention the post applied for in the subject line of the email.
Send the complete application to the official email address mentioned in the notification.
Ensure the email is sent before the last date: 16-06-2026.
Keep a copy of the sent email for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the ICAR IARI PTMO recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit your application via email is 16th June 2026. Ensure your application reaches before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. What is the age limit for the Part-time Medical Officer post?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for the Part-time Medical Officer (PTMO) post is 65 years as of 1st June 2026.

faq.question: Q3. What is the salary offered for the PTMO position?
faq.answer: The selected candidate will receive a consolidated salary of Rs. 40,000/- per month.

faq.question: Q4. What is the required qualification for the PTMO role?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess an MBBS or equivalent degree from a recognized university and be registered with the NMC or State Medical Council.

faq.question: Q5. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q6. How will candidates be selected for the PTMO post?
faq.answer: The selection process involves application screening, a walk-in interview, and document verification.

faq.question: Q7. What is the total number of vacancies available?
faq.answer: There is one vacancy for the Part-time Medical Officer (PTMO) position.

faq.question: Q8. What is the mode of application for this job?
faq.answer: The application process is through email. Candidates need to send their application and documents to the official email address.

faq.question: Q9. When is the walk-in interview scheduled?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview is scheduled for 24th June 2026. Candidates need to report for registration on the same day.

faq.question: Q10. Does ICAR IARI offer any age relaxation?
faq.answer: Age relaxation is available in exceptional cases on a case-to-case basis, but engagement will not be permitted beyond 70 years.
